Interface TextExpression

All Superinterfaces:
ComparableExpression<String>, Expression<String>, Selection<String>, TupleElement<String>
public interface TextExpression extends ComparableExpression<String>
Type for string query expressions.
Since:
4.0

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    TextExpression
    append(Expression<String> y)
    Create an expression for string concatenation.
    TextExpression
    append(String y)
    Create an expression for string concatenation.
    TextExpression
    coalesce(Expression<? extends String> y)
    Create an expression that returns null if this and the argument evaluate to null, and the value of the first non-null expression otherwise.
    TextExpression
    coalesce(String y)
    Create an expression that returns null if this and the argument evaluate to null, and the value of the first non-null expression otherwise.

    Create a predicate for testing whether this expression contains the given substring.

    Create a predicate for testing whether this expression ends with the given suffix.

    Create an expression for the leftmost substring of this string.

    Create expression to locate the position of one string within this, returning position of first character if found.

    Create expression for converting this string to lowercase.

    Create a predicate for testing whether this expression does not start with the given prefix.

    Create an expression that tests whether this expression is equal to the argument, returning null if they are and the value of the first expression if they are not.

    Create an expression that tests whether this expression is equal to the argument, returning null if they are and the value of the first expression if they are not.

      Predicate like(Expression<String> pattern)
      Create a predicate for testing whether this expression satisfies the given pattern.
      Parameters:
      pattern - string expression
      Returns:
      like predicate

      escapeChar - escape character expression
      Returns:

    • append

      TextExpression append(Expression<String> y)
      Create an expression for string concatenation.
      Parameters:
      y - string expression
      Returns:
      expression corresponding to concatenation
      See Also:
      • CriteriaBuilder.concat(Expression, Expression)

      TextExpression append(String y)
      Create an expression for string concatenation.
      Parameters:
      y - string
      Returns:

      Returns:
      expression corresponding to concatenation
      See Also:

    • substring

      TextExpression substring(Expression<Integer> from)
      Create an expression for substring extraction. Extracts a substring starting at the specified position through to end of the string. First position is 1.
      Parameters:
      from - start position expression
      Returns:
      expression corresponding to substring extraction
      See Also:
      • CriteriaBuilder.substring(Expression, Expression)

    • substring

      TextExpression substring(int from)
      Create an expression for substring extraction. Extracts a substring starting at the specified position through to end of the string. First position is 1.
      Parameters:
      from - start position
      Returns:
      expression corresponding to substring extraction
      See Also:
      • CriteriaBuilder.substring(Expression, int)

    • substring

      TextExpression substring(Expression<Integer> from, Expression<Integer> len)
      Create an expression for substring extraction. Extracts a substring of given length starting at the specified position. First position is 1.
      Parameters:
      from - start position expression
      len - length expression
      Returns:
      expression corresponding to substring extraction
      See Also:
      • CriteriaBuilder.substring(Expression, Expression, Expression)

    • substring

      TextExpression substring(int from, int len)
      Create an expression for substring extraction. Extracts a substring of given length starting at the specified position. First position is 1.
      Parameters:
      from - start position
      len - length
      Returns:
      expression corresponding to substring extraction
      See Also:
      • CriteriaBuilder.substring(Expression, int, int)

    • trim

      TextExpression trim()
      Create expression to trim blanks from both ends of this string.
      Returns:
      trim expression
      See Also:
      • CriteriaBuilder.trim(Expression)

      TextExpression trim(CriteriaBuilder.Trimspec ts)
      Create expression to trim blanks from this string.
      Parameters:
      ts - trim specification
      Returns:
      trim expression
      See Also:

      TextExpression trim(Expression<Character> t)
      Create expression to trim character from both ends of this string.
      Parameters:
      t - expression for character to be trimmed
      Returns:
      trim expression
      See Also:

      t - expression for character to be trimmed
      Returns:
      trim expression

      Parameters:
      t - character to be trimmed
      Returns:
      trim expression

      ts - trim specification
      t - character to be trimmed
      Returns:
      trim expression

      Returns:

    • replace

      TextExpression replace(Expression<String> substring, Expression<String> replacement)
      Create an expression replacing every occurrence of a substring within this string.
      Parameters:
      substring - the literal substring to replace
      replacement - the replacement string
      Returns:
      expression for the resulting string
      See Also:
      • CriteriaBuilder.replace(Expression, Expression, Expression)

    • replace

      TextExpression replace(String substring, Expression<String> replacement)
      Create an expression replacing every occurrence of a substring within this string.
      Parameters:
      substring - the literal substring to replace
      replacement - the replacement string
      Returns:
      expression for the resulting string
      See Also:
      • CriteriaBuilder.replace(Expression, String, Expression)

    • replace

      TextExpression replace(Expression<String> substring, String replacement)
      Create an expression replacing every occurrence of a substring within this string.
      Parameters:
      substring - the literal substring to replace
      replacement - the replacement string
      Returns:
      expression for the resulting string
      See Also:
      • CriteriaBuilder.replace(Expression, Expression, String)

    • replace

      TextExpression replace(String substring, String replacement)
      Create an expression replacing every occurrence of a substring within this string.
      Parameters:
      substring - the literal substring to replace
      replacement - the replacement string
      Returns:
      expression for the resulting string
      See Also:
      • CriteriaBuilder.replace(Expression, String, String)

    • locate

      NumericExpression<Integer> locate(Expression<String> pattern)
      Create expression to locate the position of one string within this, returning position of first character if found. The first position in a string is denoted by 1. If the string to be located is not found, 0 is returned.

      Warning: the order of the parameters of this method is reversed compared to the corresponding function in JPQL.

      Parameters:
      pattern - expression for string to be located
      Returns:
      expression corresponding to position
      See Also:
      • CriteriaBuilder.locate(Expression, Expression)

    • locate

      NumericExpression<Integer> locate(String pattern)
      Create expression to locate the position of one string within this, returning position of first character if found. The first position in a string is denoted by 1. If the string to be located is not found, 0 is returned.

      Warning: the order of the parameters of this method is reversed compared to the corresponding function in JPQL.

      Parameters:
      pattern - string to be located
      Returns:
      expression corresponding to position
      See Also:
      • CriteriaBuilder.locate(Expression, String)

    • locate

      NumericExpression<Integer> locate(Expression<String> pattern, Expression<Integer> from)
      Create expression to locate the position of one string within this, returning position of first character if found. The first position in a string is denoted by 1. If the string to be located is not found, 0 is returned.

      Warning: the order of the first two parameters of this method is reversed compared to the corresponding function in JPQL.

      Parameters:
      pattern - expression for string to be located
      from - expression for position at which to start search
      Returns:
      expression corresponding to position
      See Also:
      • CriteriaBuilder.locate(Expression, Expression, Expression)

    • locate

      NumericExpression<Integer> locate(String pattern, int from)
      Create expression to locate the position of one string within this, returning position of first character if found. The first position in a string is denoted by 1. If the string to be located is not found, 0 is returned.

      Warning: the order of the first two parameters of this method is reversed compared to the corresponding function in JPQL.

      Parameters:
      pattern - string to be located
      from - position at which to start search
      Returns:
      expression corresponding to position
      See Also:
      • CriteriaBuilder.locate(Expression, String, int)

      Create an expression that returns null if this and the argument evaluate to null, and the value of the first non-null expression otherwise.

      Specified by:

      Parameters:

      Returns:
